devanagari: ऐलगीतम् । श्रीभगवानुवाच । मल्लक्षणमिमं कायं लब्ध्वा मद्धर्म आस्थितः । आनन्दं परमात्मानम् आत्मस्थं समुपैति माम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: ailagītam śrībhagavānuvāca mallakṣaṇamimaṃ kāyaṃ labdhvā maddharma āsthitaḥ ānandaṃ paramātmānam ātmasthaṃ samupaiti mām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The Lord said:
He who having obtained this human body (existence) capable of perceiving my real nature, and having adopted the path of devotion so pleasing to me, duly attains to me who am Supreme Bliss and the Supreme Soul abiding in his heart.

devanagari: गुणमय्या जीवयोन्या विमुक्तो ज्ञाननिष्ठया । गुणेषु मायामात्रेषु दृश्यमानेष्ववस्तुतः । वर्तमानोऽपि न पुमान् युज्यतेऽवस्तुभिर्गुणैः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: guṇamayyā jīvayonyā vimukto jñānaniṣṭhayā guṇeṣu māyāmātreṣu dṛśyamāneṣvavastutaḥ vartamāno
translation: Emancipated completely by dint of his knowledge from the Jīva-hood constituted of guṇas, and though living in the midst of objects of senses which are unreal, he does not get attached to them, as they are created by (and hence dependent upon) Māyā (and as such) are perceived falsely.

devanagari: सङ्गं न कुर्यादसतां शिश्नोदरतृपां क्वचित् । तस्यानुगस्तमस्यन्धे पतत्यन्धानुगान्धवत् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: saṅgaṃ na kuryādasatāṃ śiśnodaratṛpāṃ kvacit tasyānugastamasyandhe patatyandhānugāndhavat
translation: One should never associate oneself with unworthy persons who seek to satiate their hunger and gratify their sexual desire. For like a blind man following another blind person, the followers of such sensualists fall into blinding darkness.

devanagari: ऐलः सम्राडिमां गाथाम् अगायत बृहच्छ्रवाः । उर्वशीविरहान् मुह्यन् निर्विण्णः शोकसंयमे ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: ailaḥ samrāḍimāṃ gāthām agāyata bṛhacchravāḥ urvaśīvirahān muhyan nirviṇṇaḥ śokasaṃyame

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ muh class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ muh class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ muh class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ muh class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ muh class 4 verb]

translation: Emperor Purūravas[1], the son of Ilā, who was well- known far and wide, became mad due to his separation from Urvaśī. When he regained his normalcy and decided to resign, he sang the following song:
purport:
Footnote [1]:
For the legend of Purūravas vide Supra IX. 14.32-47.

devanagari: त्यक्त्वाऽत्मानं व्रजन्तीं तां नग्न उन्मत्तवन्नृपः । विलपन् अन्वगाज्जाये घोरे तिष्ठेति विक्लवः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: tyaktvā vilapan anvagājjāye ghore tiṣṭheti viklavaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: As Urvaśī forsook him, the king lamented loudly and like a mad person, unaware of his nudity, followed her crying, “Wait, O cruel wife, do stop”.

devanagari: कामानतृप्तोऽनुजुषन् क्षुल्लकान् वर्षयामिनीः । न वेद यान्तीर्नायान्तीः उर्वश्याकृष्टचेतनः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: kāmānatṛpto na veda yāntīrnāyāntīḥ urvaśyākṛṣṭacetanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: His mind being completely fascinated by Urvaśī, he enjoyed trivial pleasures through nights year after year and remained unsated. He was not aware of the years that passed away nor those coming in.

devanagari: ऐल उवाच । अहो मे मोहविस्तारः कामकश्मलचेतसः । देव्या गृहीतकण्ठस्य नायुःखण्डा इमे स्मृताः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: aila uvāca aho me mohavistāraḥ kāmakaśmalacetasaḥ devyā gṛhītakaṇṭhasya nāyuḥkhaṇḍā ime smṛtāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative plural from √ dīv ], [vocative plural from √ dīv ]
[nominative plural from √ dīv ], [vocative plural from √ dīv ], [accusative plural from √ dīv ]
[nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation:
Aila (Purūravas) said:
Alas! How terrifically vast is the extent of my infatuation! My mind has been soiled (and spoilt) by my libidinousness. I was not even aware of the loss of (the major part of) my life, while my neck was being clasped by the queen (Urvaśī).

devanagari: नाहं वेदाभिनिर्मुक्तः सूर्यो वाभ्युदितोऽमुया । मूषितो वर्षपूगानां बताहानि गतान्युत ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: nāhaṃ vedābhinirmuktaḥ sūryo vābhyudito mūṣito varṣapūgānāṃ batāhāni gatānyuta

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]

translation: Hypnotized by her, I could not know when the sun arose or set. What I pity, that being enamoured of her, I was unaware how days amounting to a number of years just flitted away.

devanagari: अहो मे आत्मसम्मोहो येनात्मा योषितां कृतः । क्रीडामृगश्चक्रवर्ती नरदेवशिखामणिः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: aho me ātmasammoho yenātmā yoṣitāṃ kṛtaḥ krīḍāmṛgaścakravartī naradevaśikhāmaṇiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: hat a shocking self-delusion! I, the sovereign ruler of the world, the crest-jewel of kings, was made a pet-deer of women.

devanagari: सपरिच्छदमात्मानं हित्वा तृणमिवेश्वरम् । यान्तीं स्त्रियं चान्वगमं नग्न उन्मत्तवद् रुदन् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: saparicchadamātmānaṃ hitvā tṛṇamiveśvaram yāntīṃ striyaṃ cānvagamaṃ nagna unmattavad rudan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active first single], [aorist active first single]
[nominative single from √ rud class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ rud class 2 verb]

translation: The woman who spurned me (contemptuously) like a blade of straw even though I was a ruler with royal grandeur (and paraphernalia). And I, weeping like a madman, went after her in nakedness.

devanagari: कुतस्तस्यानुभावः स्यात् तेज ईशत्वमेव वा । योऽन्वगच्छं स्त्रियं यान्तीं खरवत् पादताडितः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: kutastasyānubhāvaḥ syāt teja īśatvameva vā yo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ru class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ ru class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ru class 1 verb]

translation: What majestic lustre, power or Lordship could there be in me, who like a donkey kicked (by a she-donkey) followed the woman as she deserted me.

devanagari: किं विद्यया किं तपसा किं त्यागेन श्रुतेन वा । किं विविक्तेन मौनेन स्त्रीभिर्यस्य मनो हृतम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ vidyayā kiṃ tapasā kiṃ tyāgena śrutena vā kiṃ viviktena maunena strībhiryasya mano hṛtam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb]

translation: What is the value of that learning, penance, liberality, scriptural scholarship, resort to solitude or observance of silence in a man who has lost his heart to women.

devanagari: स्वार्थस्याकोविदं धिङ् मां मूर्खं पण्डितमानिनम् । योऽहमीश्वरतां प्राप्य स्त्रीभिर्गोखरवज्जितः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: svārthasyākovidaṃ dhiṅ māṃ mūrkhaṃ paṇḍitamāninam yo
translation: Fie upon me I A fool who, though impervious to his self-interest, fancy myself to be a learned man and who, even after being installed as a sovereign ruler, was subdued and roped like a bull or a donkey by women.

devanagari: सेवतो वर्षपूगान् मे उर्वश्या अधरासवम् । न तृप्यत्यात्मभूः कामो वह्निराहुतिभिर्यथा ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: sevato varṣapūgān me urvaśyā adharāsavam na tṛpyatyātmabhūḥ kāmo vahnirāhutibhiryathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ tṛp class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ tṛp class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ tṛp class 4 verb], [locative single from √ tṛp class 4 verb]

translation: Although I had been enjoying the intoxicating nectar of the lips of Urvaśī for years together, my inborn lust for her is not satiated, like fire fed with oblations (of ghee)[2].
purport:
Footnote [2]:
The famous verse attributed to King Yayāti in Mahābhārata Ādi. 85.12.
na jātu kāmaḥ kāmānām upabhogena śāmyati /
haviṣā kṛṣṇa-vartmeva bhūya evā'bhivardhate //

devanagari: पुंश्चल्यापहृतं चित्तं को न्वन्यो मोचितुं प्रभुः । आत्मारामेश्वरमृते भगवन्तम् अधोक्षजम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: puṃścalyāpahṛtaṃ cittaṃ ko nvanyo mocituṃ prabhuḥ ātmārāmeśvaramṛte bhagavantam adhokṣajam
translation: Who else than the glorious Lord Viṣṇu, the Lord of those who revel in the spiritual bliss of the Soul, is capable to deliver my mind from (the clutches) of a wench who captivated it.

devanagari: बोधितस्यापि देव्या मे सूक्तवाक्येन दुर्मतेः । मनोगतो महामोहो नापयात्यजितात्मनः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: bodhitasyāpi devyā me sūktavākyena durmateḥ manogato mahāmoho nāpayātyajitātmanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ dīv ], [vocative plural from √ dīv ]
[nominative plural from √ dīv ], [vocative plural from √ dīv ], [accusative plural from √ dīv ]

translation: Even though I was advised by the queen (Urvaśī) in well-argued speech (recorded in a Vedic Sūkta) the deep infatuation entrenched in my mind does not disappear, as I, being perverse-minded, had not controlled my mind.

devanagari: किमेतया नोऽपकृतं रज्ज्वा वा सर्पचेतसः । रज्जु स्वरूपाविदुषो योऽहं यदजितेन्द्रियः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: kimetayā no rajju svarūpāviduṣo yo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: What offence has been committed by her against me? Or what wrong can be attributed to a rope against a person who, not knowing its real nature, mistook it for a serpent? It is I who could not subdue my senses, am responsible (for this tragedy).

devanagari: क्वायं मलीमसः कायो दौर्गन्ध्याद्यात्मकोऽशुचिः । क्व गुणाः सौमनस्याद्या ह्यध्यासोऽविद्यया कृतः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: kvāyaṃ malīmasaḥ kāyo daurgandhyādyātmako kva guṇāḥ saumanasyādyā hyadhyāso

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: What a lot of difference there is between a (woman’s or a human) body full of dirt, stinking with bad odour and impure and the attributes of being like a flower (clean sweet-smelling, pure). But these attributes are superimposed on it (a woman’s body) through Nescience.

devanagari: पित्रोः किं स्वं नु भार्यायाः स्वामिनोऽग्नेः श्वगृध्रयोः । किमात्मनः किं सुहृदां इति यो नावसीयते ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: pitroḥ kiṃ svaṃ nu bhāryāyāḥ svāmino kimātmanaḥ kiṃ suhṛdāṃ iti yo nāvasīyate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [ablative single from √ bhṛ ], [genitive single from √ bhṛ ]

translation: It cannot be definitely determined whether this human body belongs to parents (who procreated it), or to the wife (one’s life-parttier), or to the employer, or to the fire (which ultimately consumes it) or to dogs and vultures (in case it is not cremated but lies unclaimed) or whether it belongs to the Soul (who abides in it) or to friends and well- wishers?

devanagari: तस्मिन् कलेवरेऽमेध्ये तुच्छनिष्ठे विषज्जते । अहो सुभद्रं सुनसं सुस्मितं च मुखं स्त्रियः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tasmin kalevare aho subhadraṃ sunasaṃ susmitaṃ ca mukhaṃ striyaḥ
translation: What a pity that a man should be fascinated with this disgusting body which disintegrates into worms (if interred), faeces (if eaten by carnivorous animals), and ashes (if cremated), and appreciates it as a beautiful feminine countenance with shapely nose, beaming with charming smiles.

devanagari: त्वङ्मांसरुधिरस्नायु मेदोमज्जास्थिसंहतौ । विण्मूत्रपूये रमतां कृमीणां कियदन्तरम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tvaṅmāṃsarudhirasnāyu medomajjāsthisaṃhatau viṇmūtrapūye ramatāṃ kṛmīṇāṃ kiyadantaram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: What difference is there between people like me who enjoy pleasure in a feminine body which is nothing but an accumulation of skin, flesh, blood, nerves, fat, marrow and bones and worms who enjoy themselves in feces, urine and pus.

devanagari: अथापि नोपसज्जेत स्त्रीषु स्त्रैणेषु चार्थवित् । विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगान् मनः क्षुभ्यति नान्यथा ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: athāpi nopasajjeta strīṣu straiṇeṣu cārthavit viṣayendriyasaṃyogān manaḥ kṣubhyati nānyathā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: A person knowing his personal interest, should under no circumstances associate himself with women or persons addicted to women; for the mind gets agitated only when there is a contact between objects of senses and senses and not otherwise.

devanagari: अदृष्टाद् अश्रुताद्‍भावात् न भाव उपजायते । असम्प्रयुञ्जतः प्राणान् शाम्यति स्तिमितं मनः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: adṛṣṭād aśrutād‍bhāvāt na bhāva upajāyate asamprayuñjataḥ prāṇān śāmyati stimitaṃ manaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ stim class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ stim class 4 verb]

translation: A thing that is not spoken about, seen or heard creates no passion or desire for it. A person who does not direct his life-breath (mind or sense-organs to external objects), gets his mind steady and serene.

devanagari: तस्मात्सङ्गो न कर्तव्यः स्त्रीषु स्त्रैणेषु चेन्द्रियैः । विदुषां चाप्यविस्रब्धः षड्वर्गः किमु मादृशाम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmātsaṅgo na kartavyaḥ strīṣu straiṇeṣu cendriyaiḥ viduṣāṃ cāpyavisrabdhaḥ ṣaḍvargaḥ kimu mādṛśām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ kṛ class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 6 verb]

translation: Therefore, one should not associate with women or women addicts through the medium of senses. For the group of six (internal) enemies (like lust, anger, greed and others) cannot be trusted even in the case of wise people. What need be said of (ignorant) persons like me?

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । एवं प्रगायन् नृपदेवदेवः । स उर्वशीलोकमथो विहाय । आत्मानमात्मन्यवगम्य मां वै । उपारमत् ज्ञानविधूतमोहः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca evaṃ pragāyan nṛpadevadevaḥ sa urvaśīlokamatho vihāya ātmānamātmanyavagamya māṃ vai upāramat jñānavidhūtamohaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation:
The Lord resumed:
Singing loudly the song (of his life) in this way, Purūravas, the King of the Kings, left the realm of Urvaśī. He realized me as his own self, and having shaken off delusion by virtue of this knowledge, he retired from worldly life.

devanagari: ततो दुःसङ्गमुत्सृज्य सत्सु सज्जेत बुद्धिमान् । सन्त एवास्य छिन्दन्ति मनोव्यासङ्गमुक्तिभिः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tato duḥsaṅgamutsṛjya satsu sajjeta buddhimān santa evāsya chindanti manovyāsaṅgamuktibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [optative active second plural], [optative middle third single]

translation: Therefore, an intelligent person should dissociate himself from evil company, and associate himself with the saintly people; for it is these pious saints who would cut off his deep attachment (to sense-objects) by their wise precepts.

devanagari: सन्तोऽनपेक्षा मच्चित्ताः प्रशान्ताः समदर्शिनः । निर्ममा निरहङ्कारा निर्द्वन्द्वा निष्परिग्रहाः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: santo nirmamā nirahaṅkārā nirdvandvā niṣparigrahāḥ
translation: The saints devote their minds to me without expecting anything in return. Being desireless, they are serene, viewing all beings as equals (as they are none else but Brahman). They have superseded the notions of mine-ness and I-ness (i.e. identification of the Soul with the body). They are not affected by the pair of opposites (like joy and sorrow, pleasure and pain) and are perfect have-nots (property-less).

devanagari: तेषु नित्यं महाभाग महाभागेषु मत्कथाः । सम्भवन्ति हिता नृणां जुषतां प्रपुनन्त्यघम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣu nityaṃ mahābhāga mahābhāgeṣu matkathāḥ sambhavanti hitā nṛṇāṃ juṣatāṃ prapunantyagham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ hi class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ hi class 5 verb]
[nominative plural from √ hi class 5 verb], [vocative plural from √ hi class 5 verb], [accusative plural from √ hi class 5 verb]

[nominative dual from √ pū class 9 verb], [nominative plural from √ pū class 9 verb], [vocative dual from √ pū class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ pū class 9 verb], [accusative dual from √ pū class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ pū class 9 verb]

translation: It is in the company of these supremely fortunate persons, O highly blessed Uddhava, that my legends are told and these stories absolve the listeners from all sins.

devanagari: ता ये श्रृण्वन्ति गायन्ति ह्यनुमोदन्ति चादृताः । मत्पराः श्रद्दधानाश्च भक्तिं विन्दन्ति ते मयि ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tā ye śrṛṇvanti gāyanti hyanumodanti cādṛtāḥ matparāḥ śraddadhānāśca bhaktiṃ vindanti te mayi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ ud class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ ud class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ ud class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vid class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ vid class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ vid class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vid class 7 verb], [vocative plural from √ vid class 7 verb], [accusative plural from √ vid class 7 verb]

translation: Those persons who listen to these (my) stories, extol them in songs, devoutly appreciate them and are devoted to me with faith and reverence, get devotion engendered in them.

devanagari: भक्तिं लब्धवतः साधोः किमन्यद् अवशिष्यते । मय्यनन्तगुणे ब्रह्मण्यानन्दानुभवात्मनि ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: bhaktiṃ labdhavataḥ sādhoḥ kimanyad avaśiṣyate mayyanantaguṇe brahmaṇyānandānubhavātmani

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ labh class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ labh class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ labh class 1 verb]
[ablative single from √ labh class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ labh class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ vā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ vā class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vā class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ vā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vā class 1 verb]

translation: What else remains to be achieved by the saints who attained real devotion towards me, the Brahman, who am endowed with infinite qualities and potencies and who am conscious blissfulness incarnate.

devanagari: यथोपश्रयमाणस्य भगवन्तं विभावसुम् । शीतं भयं तमोऽप्येति साधून् संसेवतस्तथा ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: yathopaśrayamāṇasya bhagavantaṃ vibhāvasum śītaṃ bhayaṃ tamo

[nominative single from √ śī class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ śī class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ śyā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ śyā class 1 verb]

translation: Just as cold, fear (of darkness) and darkness fly away from a person who resorts to the fire, similarly, those who wait upon and worship saints, become free from fear, ignorance and apathy.

devanagari: निमज्ज्योन्मज्जतां घोरे भवाब्धौ परमायनम् । सन्तो ब्रह्मविदः शान्ता नौर्दृढेवाप्सु मज्जताम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: nimajjyonmajjatāṃ ghore bhavābdhau paramāyanam santo brahmavidaḥ śāntā naurdṛḍhevāpsu majjatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 9 verb]

[nominative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ śam class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation: Even as a strong boat is to those who are drowningin the sea, saints, serene-minded knowers of the Brahman are the best life-savers to those who are tossed about and are drowning in the sea of Saṃsāra.

devanagari: अन्नं हि प्राणिनां प्राण आर्तानां शरणं त्वहम् । धर्मो वित्तं नृणां प्रेत्य सन्तोऽर्वाग् बिभ्यतोऽरणम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: annaṃ hi prāṇināṃ prāṇa ārtānāṃ śaraṇaṃ tvaham dharmo vittaṃ nṛṇāṃ pretya santo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vid class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ vid class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 7 verb]

translation: Just as food is the very life of living beings, I am the shelter to the afflicted. Righteousness (pious acts) is the wealth (investment) in the other world, the saints are the support (resort) of those who are afraid of repeated transmigration of the Soul in future.

devanagari: सन्तो दिशन्ति चक्षूंषि बहिरर्कः समुत्थितः । देवता बान्धवाः सन्तः सन्त आत्माहमेव च ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: santo diśanti cakṣūṃṣi bahirarkaḥ samutthitaḥ devatā bāndhavāḥ santaḥ santa ātmāhameva ca

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ sam class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ sam class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ sam class 1 verb], [locative single from √ sam class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sam class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sam class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ sam class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ sam class 1 verb]

translation: The saints confer the (inner) vision (of the Brahman) on those who associate with them, even as the sun bestows the external eye (to see nature). Saints are deities, the near and dear relatives, the very soul, nay they are even myself.

devanagari: वैतसेनस्ततोऽप्येवम् उर्वश्या लोकनिस्पृहः । मुक्तसङ्गो महीमेतम् आत्मारामश्चचार ह ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: vaitasenastato muktasaṅgo mahīmetam ātmārāmaścacāra ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
It is traditionally recounted that Purūravas who divested himself of all longing for the realm of Urvaśī (and pleasures in her company), renounced all attachment, and revelling in the spiritual bliss of the Ātman, went about all over the world.
